hadiths.chapter: 65. THE TIME FOR NIGHT PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1383 - وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْحَّسَّن ُّ إَّلص َّي ْق َّلُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال ى  ق َّي َّسْا َّلُّن ِى  عَّن ْ 
عَّمَّلِ رَّسُّولِ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ ق َّي َّق ُّولُّ إ َّر ِي ذُّ كَّا َّن  َّهُّ ي َّزَّى إ َّن  َّ رَّسُّولَّ إَّل ل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ ق َّص َّزَّ ق ِى  س َّى ْءٍ 
وَّ إ ِن ِّى  لَّأ َّمْق ُّث ُّ إَّلز َّج ُّلَّ ق َّذْ ق َّزَّإ َّ إَّلْق ُّزْإ ن َّ ي ُّم َّ ي َّسْي َّي ْق ِظ ُّ مِن َّ إَّلل َّي ْلِ ق َّلاَّ ي َّق ُّومُّ جَّي  َّى إ ِذ َّإ كَّان َّ عِ ن ْذَّ إَّلص ُّن ْحِ ق َّامَّ ي ُّن َّاذِرُّةُّ ن ِصَّلاَّي ِهِ .
hadiths.en:
Al-'Ala' reported from Muhammad ibn Muslim from one of the Imams {a.s} who 
said:  
"There is no servant except that they are awakened during the night once or twice. If 
they rise (for prayer), it will be for their benefit; otherwise, Shaytan comes and urinates 
in their ear. Do you not see that when one rises and has not risen for prayer, they do so 
sluggishly and heavily, feeling lazy?" 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 478 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 99 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 65, H. 1382
reference.chapter: THE TIME FOR NIGHT PRAYER
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE TIME FOR NIGHT PRAYER

hadiths.chapter: 65. THE TIME FOR NIGHT PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1384  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إ َّن ُّو جَّمْز َّة َّ إَّلي  ُّمَّالِى  ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال   إ َّي  َّه َّ سَّاعَّه ٍ ن َّوَّى ق َّعَّلِمَّ 
إَّلل َّهُّ ي َّن َّارَّك َّ وي َّعَّالَّى ذ َّلِك َّ مِن ْهُّ إ ِلأ َّ وَّك َّلَّ ن ِهِ مَّلَّكَّي ْن ِ ي ُّحَّزِّكَّان ِهِ ي ِلْك َّ إَّلس َّاعَّه َّ .
hadiths.en:
Al-Hasan al -Sayqal reported from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} 
who said: "I despise a man who comes to me asking about the actions of the 
Messenger of Allah {saws} and then says, 'Should I add more?' as if he sees that the 
Messenger of Allah {saws} was lacking in something.  
And I despise a man who has read the Qur'an and then wakes up during the night but 
does not rise (for prayer) until, when it is near dawn, he hastily performs his prayer."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 479 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 77 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 65, H. 1383

hadiths.chapter: 65. THE TIME FOR NIGHT PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1385  -  وَّ رَّوَّى عِي ضُّ ن ْن ُّ إَّلْق َّاسِمِ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال وْمُّ وَّ هُّوَّ ق ِى  إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ 
ق َّلْي َّص َّغْ رَّإ ْسَّهُّ ق َّلْي َّن َّمْ ق َّا ِن ِّى  إ َّي َّح َّو َّف ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إ ِن ْ إ َّرَّإذَّ إ َّن ْ ي َّق ُّولَّ إَّلل َّهُّم َّ إ َّ ذْج ِلْن ِى  إَّلْح َّن  َّه َّ إ َّن ْ ي َّق ُّولَّ إَّلل َّهُّم َّ إ َّذْج ِلْن ِى  إَّلن  َّارَّ .
hadiths.en:
Abu Hamzah al-Thumali reported from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s} 
who said:  
"There is no servant who intends to rise (for prayer) at any hour they intend, and Allah 
{SWT}, Blessed and Exalted, knows this intention from them, except that He assigns two 
angels to move them at that hour." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 479 • Falah Al -Sael, Part 1, p. 286 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 
100 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 162 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 73, p. 215 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 65, H. 1384

reference.location: Volume 1 › THE TIME FOR NIGHT PRAYER

hadiths.chapter: 65. THE TIME FOR NIGHT PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1386  -  وَّ رَّوَّى ر َّكَّزِي  َّا إَّلن  َّق  َّاض ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  ق ِى  ق َّوْلِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل 
سُّكٰارىٰ جَّي ّٰى ي َّعْلَّمُّوإ مٰا ي َّق ُّولُّون  ق َّال .
hadiths.en:
Ays ibn al-Qasim reported from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} who 
said:  
"If a man is overcome by sleep while he is in prayer, let him put his head down and 
sleep, for I fear that if he wishes to say, 'O’ Allah {SWT}, admit me to Paradise,' he might 
instead say, 'O’ Allah {SWT}, admit me to the Fire.'" 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 479 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 844 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 7, p. 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 65, H. 1385
reference.chapter: THE TIME FOR NIGHT PRAYER
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE TIME FOR NIGHT PRAYER

hadiths.chapter: 65. THE TIME FOR NIGHT PRAYER
hadiths.en:
Zakariyya al -Naqqad reported from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s} 
regarding the words of Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic:  
"Do not approach prayer while you are intoxicated until you know what you are saying" 
(Surah An-Nisa 4:43).  
Imam {a.s} said: "This includes the intoxication of sleep." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 479 • Wasail Al-
Shia, Part 7, p. 291 • Tafsir Nur Al-Thaqalayn, Part 1, p. 483 • Tafseer Kanz Al-Daqaiq, Part 3, p. 412

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 65, H. 1386

hadiths.chapter: 66. WHAT A PERSON SHOULD SAY UPON WAKING FROM
hadiths.ar:
1388  -  وَّ رَّوَّى ج َّز َّإح إَّلْمَّذَّإن ِن ِى  ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال   إَّلل َّهِ رَّب ِّ  
إَّلن  َّن ِي ِّي ن َّ وَّ إ ِلَّهِ إَّلْمُّزْسَّلِي ن َّ وَّ رَّب ِّ إَّلْمُّسْي َّص ْعَّق ِي ن َّ وَّ إَّلْحَّمْذُّ لِل َّهِ إَّل َّذ ِى  ي ُّحْى ِ إَّلْمَّوْ ي ىٰ وَّ هُّوَّ عَّلىٰ كُّلِّ س َّى ْءٍ ق َّذِي ز   ق َّا ِن  َّهُّ إ ِذ َّإ  
ق َّالَّ ذ َّلِك َّ ي َّق ُّولُّ إَّلل َّهُّ ي َّن َّارَّك َّ وي َّعَّالَّى  صَّذَّق َّ عَّن ْذِى  وَّ س َّكَّزَّ .
hadiths.en:
When the Messenger of Allah {saws} would go to his bed, he would say:  
"In Your name, O’ Allah {SWT}, I live, and in Your name, I die."  
And when he would wake up, he would say:  
"All praise is due to Allah {SWT}, who brought me back to life after causing me to die, and 
to Him is the return." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 480 • Meftah al-Falah, Part 1, p. 284 • Bihar Al-Anwar, 
Vol. 84, p. 173 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 66, H. 1387
reference.chapter: WHAT A PERSON SHOULD SAY UPON WAKING FROM
reference.location: Volume 1 › WHAT A PERSON SHOULD SAY UPON WAKING FROM
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٦، ح ١٣٨٧

hadiths.chapter: 66. WHAT A PERSON SHOULD SAY UPON WAKING FROM
hadiths.ar:
1389  -  وَّ رَّوَّى عَّن ْذُّ إَّلز َّجْمَّن ِ ن ْن ُّ إَّلْحَّح  َّاح ِ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :  «
جَّي  َّى ي َّسْمَّغَّ إ َّهْلُّ إَّلذ َّإرِ وَّ ي َّق ُّولُّ: إَّلل َّهُّم َّ إ َّعِن ِّى  عَّلَّى هَّوْلِ إَّلْمُّط َّلَّغِ وَّ وَّسِّغْ عَّلَّى  َّ إَّلْمَّص ْح َّغ وَّ إُّرْر ُّق ْن ِى  ج َّي ْزَّ مَّا ق َّن ْلَّ إَّلْمَّوْب ِ 
وَّ إُّرْر ُّق ْن ِى  ج َّي ْزَّ مَّا ن َّعْذَّ إَّلْمَّوْب ِ .
hadiths.en:
Jarraḥ al-Mada'ini reported from Abu Abdullah {a.s} who said: "When one of you 
rises (from sleep), let him say:  
'Glory be to Allah {SWT}, the Lord {AZJ} of the Prophets, the God of the Messengers, and the 
Lord {AZJ} of the oppressed. All praise is due to Allah {SWT}, who gives life to the dead and 
who has power over all things.'  
When he says this, Allah {SWT}, Blessed and Exalted, says,  
'My servant has spoken the truth and expressed gratitude.'"  
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Volume 2, p. 538 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 480 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 9, p. 1591 
• Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 84, p. 187 • Mustadrak Al-Wasail, Vol. 4, p. 151 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 66, H. 1388
reference.chapter: WHAT A PERSON SHOULD SAY UPON WAKING FROM

hadiths.chapter: 66. WHAT A PERSON SHOULD SAY UPON WAKING FROM
hadiths.ar:
1390  -  وَّ ق ِى  ج َّن َّزٍ إ ج َّزَّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال 
إَّلْحَّمْذُّ لِل َّهِ إَّل َّذ ِى  رَّذ َّ عَّلَّى  َّ رُّوجِى  إ َّعْن ُّذُّةُّ وَّ إ َّجْمَّذُّةُّ إَّلل َّهُّم َّ إ ِن  َّهُّ لأَّ ي ُّوَّإرِى  مِن ْك َّ لَّي ْل  سَّاح ٍ  وَّ لأَّ سَّمَّاء  ذ َّ إب ُّ إ َّن ْزَّإح ٍ وَّ لأَّ إ َّرْض   
ذ َّإب ُّ مِهَّاذٍ وَّ لأ   ن َّعْص ُّهٰا ق َّوْق َّ ن َّعْض ٍ   وَّ لأَّ ن َّحْز  لُّح ِّى    ي ُّذْلِح ُّ ن َّي ْن َّ ي َّذَّى ِ إَّلْمُّذْلِح ِ مِن ْ ج َّلْق ِك َّ ي َّعْلَّم 
وَّ مٰا ي ُّح ْق ِى  إَّلص ُّذُّور   ع َّارَّب ِ إَّلن  ُّح ُّومُّ وَّ ن َّامَّث ِ إَّ لْعُّي ُّون ُّ وَّ إ َّن ْث   لأَّ ي َّا ْج ُّذ ُّك   «
إَّللّٰهِ رَّب ِّ إَّلْعٰالَّمِي ن   وَّ إ ِلَّهِ إَّلْمُّزْسَّلِي ن َّ وَّ ج َّالِق ِ إَّلن  َّن ِي ِّي ن   إَّلل َّهُّم َّ إِع ْق ِزْ لِى  وَّ إِرْجَّمْن ِى  وَّ ي ُّث ْ  
عَّلَّى   ي ُّم َّ إِق ْزَّإ ْ ج َّمْسَّ إ ي َّاب ٍ مِن ْ إ ج ِزِ إ لِ عِمْزَّإن   إ ِلَّى  
ق َّوْلِهِ  
وعلي ك  ن السوإك  ق ا ن  إلسوإك  ق ى  إلسحز ق ن ل إلوص وء من  إلسن ه ، ي م ي وص ا  .
hadiths.en:
Abd al -Rahman ibn al -Hajjaj reports from Imam Al -Sadiq {a.s} that when he 
would rise in the last part of the night, he would raise his voice such that the people of 
the household could hear and say: "O’ Allah {SWT}, help me with the terror of the Rising 
(on the Day of Judgment), make my resting place spacious, grant me the best of what 
comes before death, and grant me the best of what comes after death."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, V. 1, p. 480 • Al-Wafi, V. 9, p. 1592 • Bihar Al-Anwar, V. 84, p. 192 
﴿HADITH﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 66, H. 1389

reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٦، ح ١٣٨٩

hadiths.chapter: 66. WHAT A PERSON SHOULD SAY UPON WAKING FROM
hadiths.ar:
1391  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إ َّن ُّو عُّن َّي ْذَّة َّ إَّلْحَّذ  َّإءُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :  ق ِى  ق َّوْلِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل 
إَّلْمَّص ٰاج ِغِ   ق َّق َّال  لِهَّذ َّإ إَّلْن َّذَّن ِ إ َّن ْ 
ي ُّزِي حَّهُّ جَّي  َّى ي َّح ْزُّح َّ ن َّق ْسُّهُّ ق َّا ِذ َّإ ج َّزَّح َّ إَّلن  َّق ْسُّ إِسْي َّزَّإحَّ إَّلْن َّذَّن ُّ وَّ رَّج َّعَّث ِ إَّلز ُّوحُّ  ق ِي هِ وَّ ق ِي هِ ق ُّو َّة   عَّلَّى إَّلْعَّمَّلِ ق َّا ِن  َّمَّا  
ذ َّكَّزَّهُّمْ ق َّق َّال   إ ُّن ْز ِلَّث ْ ق ِى   إ َّمِي زِ إَّلْمُّو ْمِن ِي ن َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ 
وَّ إ َّي ْن َّاعِهِ مِن ْ س ِي عَّي ِن َّا ي َّن َّامُّون َّ ق ِى  إ َّو َّلِ إَّلل َّي ْلِ ق َّا ِذ َّإ ذ َّهَّث َّ ي ُّلُّي َّا إَّلل َّي ْلِ إ َّوْ مَّا س َّاءَّ إَّلل َّهُّ ق َّ ز ِعُّوإ إ ِلَّى رَّن ِّهِمْ رَّإع ِن ِي ن َّ رَّإهِن ِي ن َّ 
طَّامِعِي ن َّ ق ِي مَّا عِن ْذَّةُّ ق َّذ َّكَّزَّهُّمُّ إَّ لل َّهُّ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ ق ِى  كِي َّان ِهِ لِن َّن ِي ِّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ وَّ إ َّج ْن َّزَّهُّمْ ن ِمَّا إ َّعْطَّاهُّمْ وَّ إ َّن  َّهُّ إ َّسْكَّن َّهُّ مْ ق ِى   
ج ِوَّإرِةِ وَّ إ َّذْج َّلَّهُّمْ ج َّن  َّي َّهُّ وَّ إ مَّن َّ ج َّوْق َّهُّمْ وَّ إ مَّن َّ رَّوْعَّي َّهُّمْ   إ ج ِزِ إَّلل َّي ْلِ إ َّى  َّ س َّى ْءٍ 
إ َّق ُّولُّ إ ِذ َّإ ق ُّمْث ُّ ق َّق َّال   وَّ إ ِلَّهِ إَّلْمُّزْسَّلِي ن َّ وَّ إَّلْحَّمْذُّ لِل َّهِ إَّل َّذ ِى   و
مَّن ْ ق ِى  إَّلْق ُّن ُّورِ  ق َّا ِن  َّك َّ إ ِذ َّإ ق ُّلْي َّهَّا ذ َّهَّث َّ عَّن ْك َّ رِج ْز ُّ إَّلس  َّي ْطَّان ِ وَّ وَّسْوَّإسُّهُّ إ ِن ْ س َّاءَّ إَّلل َّهُّ ي َّعَّالَّى .
hadiths.en:
In another report from Abu Ja'far {a.s}, He said:  
"When you rise from your bed, look to the horizon of the sky and say:  
'All praise is due to Allah {SWT}, who has returned my soul to me; I worship Him and 
praise Him. O’ Allah {SWT}, neither a dark night nor a sky full of constellations nor an 
earth with layers nor layers of darkness nor a vast sea that flows can conceal from You. 
You know the betrayal of the eyes and what the hearts conceal. The stars have set, and 
the eyes have clo sed, yet You are the Living, the Self -subsisting, whom neither 
slumber nor sleep overtakes. Glory be to Allah {SWT}, the Lord {AZJ} of the worlds, the God 
of the Messengers, the Creator of the Prophets, and all praise is due to Him, Allah {SWT}, 
the Lord {AZJ} of the worlds. O’ Allah {SWT}, forgive me, have mercy on me, and accept my 
repentance; surely, You are the Most Accepting of Repentance, the Most Merciful.'  
Then recite the last five verses of Surah Al-Imran:  
Inna fī khalqi as-samāwāti wa al-arḍi ' to Innaka lā tukhlifu al-mī‘ād "  
[AL SADUQ] 
He also emphasized using a siwak (tooth -stick) before ablution during the pre -dawn 
hours, as it is a sunnah, then perform ablution.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 66, H. 1390
reference.chapter: WHAT A PERSON SHOULD SAY UPON WAKING FROM
reference.location: Volume 1 › WHAT A PERSON SHOULD SAY UPON WAKING FROM
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٦، ح ١٣٩٠

hadiths.chapter: 66. WHAT A PERSON SHOULD SAY UPON WAKING FROM
hadiths.en:
Abu Ubaydah al-Haddha’ reported from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} 
regarding the verse of Allah {SWT}, the Exalted,  
"Their sides forsake their beds" (Surah As-Sajdah 32:16).  
Abu Ja’far {a.s} said: "Perhaps you think that these people did not sleep?"  
I replied: "Allah {SWT} and His Messenger know best."  
Imam {a.s} said: "This body must be allowed rest so that the soul can depart and the 
body can rejuvenate. When the soul returns, it restores the body's strength for action.  
This verse refers to those who rest their bodies but then rise in devotion, praying to 
their Lord {AZJ} out of fear and hope.  
This was revealed concerning The Commander of the Faithful {a.s} and his followers 
among our Shia. They sleep in the early part of the night, then when two -thirds of the 
night have passed or as much as Allah {SWT} wills, they hasten to their Lord {AZJ}, desiring 
Him, fearing Him, and hoping for what He has. Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic, 
mentioned them in His Book to His Prophet {saws} and informed them of what He had 
granted them - settling them near Him, entering them into His Paradise, granting them 
security from fear, and calming their dread." 
 I said: "May I be your ransom, if I rise during the last part of the night, what should I 
say?"  
Imam {a.s} said: "Say, 'All praise is due to Allah {SWT}, the Lord {AZJ} of the worlds, the God 
of the Messengers. All praise is due to Allah {SWT}, who gives life to the dead and 
resurrects those in the graves.' When you say this, the evil whisperings of Shaytan will 
depart from you, if Allah {SWT} wills." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 481 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 
9, p. 1593 • Tafsir Al-Burhan, vol. 4, p. 393

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 66, H. 1391

hadiths.chapter: 67. THE SAYING WHEN THE ROOSTER CROWS
hadiths.ar:
1393  -  وَّق َّال عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم   إَّلس َّح َّاءَّ 
وَّ إَّلس  َّح َّاعَّه َّ وَّ كَّي ْزَّة َّ إَّلط َّزُّوق َّه ِ .
hadiths.en:
Imam Al-Sadiq {a.s} said: "When you hear the crowing of the rooster, say:  
'Glorified and Holy is the Lord {AZJ} of the angels and the spirit; Your mercy has preceded 
Your wrath; there is no god but You; glory and praise be to You. I have acted badly and 
wronged myself, so forgive me. Indeed, no one forgives sins except You.'".  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 482 • Makarem al -Akhlaq, Part 1, p. 293 • Meftah al -
Falah, Part 1, p. 285 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 9, p. 1593 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 20, p. 863 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 6, p. 35 • Bihar 
Al Anwar, Vol. 84, p. 184 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 67, H. 1392
reference.chapter: THE SAYING WHEN THE ROOSTER CROWS
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE SAYING WHEN THE ROOSTER CROWS
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٧، ح ١٣٩٢

hadiths.chapter: 67. THE SAYING WHEN THE ROOSTER CROWS
hadiths.en:
Imam {a.s} said: "Learn five qualities from the rooster: its adherence to the 
timing of prayers, its protective jealousy (for its mate), its generosity, its courage, and 
its frequent crowing (indicating vigilance)”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 482 • Makarem al -Akhlaq, Part 1, p. 293 • Wasail Al-
Shia, Vol. 20, p. 242 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 62, p. 3 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 67, H. 1393

reference.location: Volume 1 › THE SAYING WHEN THE ROOSTER CROWS

hadiths.chapter: 67. THE SAYING WHEN THE ROOSTER CROWS
hadiths.ar:
1395  -  وَّق َّال إ َّن ُّو ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «وي َّعَّالَّىمَّلَّكاً عَّلَّى صُّورَّة ِ ذِي ك ٍ إ َّن ْي َّض َّ رَّإ ْسُّهُّ ي َّحْث َّ إَّلْعَّزْس ِ وَّ 
رِج ْلاَّةُّ ق ِى  ي ُّح ُّومِ إَّلْأ َّرْض ِ إَّلس َّان ِعَّه ِ لَّهُّ ج َّن َّاح  ق ِى  إَّلْمَّس ْزِق ِ وَّ ج َّن َّاح  ق ِى  إَّلْمَّع ْزِب ِ لأَّ ي َّصِي حُّ إَّلذ ُّي ُّ وك ُّ جَّي  َّى ي َّصِي حَّ ق َّا ِذ َّإ 
صَّاحَّ ج َّق َّق َّ ن ِح َّن َّاجَّي ْهِ ي ُّم َّ ق َّالَّ  سُّن ْحَّان َّ إَّلل َّهِ سُّن ْحَّان َّ إَّلل َّهِ سُّن ْحَّان َّ إَّلل َّهِ إَّلْعَّط ِي مِ إَّل َّذ ِى   ق َّالَّ ق َّي ُّح ِي ن ُّهُّ 
إَّلل َّهُّ ي َّن َّارَّك َّ وي َّعَّالَّى وَّ ي َّق ُّولُّ لأَّ ي َّحْلِف ُّ  ن ِى  كَّاذ ِن اً مَّن ْ ي َّعْزِف ُّ مَّا ي َّق ُّولُّ .
hadiths.en:
Imam {a.s} said: "Learn three qualities from the crow: its concealment when 
mating, its early rising in seeking sustenance, and its cautiousness."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 482 • Al-Khasal, Part 1, p. 99 • Uyūn al-Akhbār, Part 1, 
p. 257 • Al-Waezin, volume 2, page 456 • Makarem al-Akhlaq, Part 1, p. 293 • Kashf al-Ghummah, Part 2, p. 
293 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 4, p. 266 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 17, p. 78 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 20, p. 133 • Wasail Al-Shia, 
Vol. 20, p. 133 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 67, H. 1394

reference.location: Volume 1 › THE SAYING WHEN THE ROOSTER CROWS
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٧، ح ١٣٩٤

hadiths.chapter: 67. THE SAYING WHEN THE ROOSTER CROWS
hadiths.ar: 1396  - وَّ رُّوِى إَّلط َّي ْزُّ صَّاق ّٰاب ٍ كُّل   ق َّذْ عَّلِمَّ صَّلاٰي َّهُّ وَّ ي َّسْن ِي حَّه  »
hadiths.en:
Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} said:  
"Indeed, Allah {SWT}, Blessed and Exalted, has an angel in the form of a white rooster. Its 
head is beneath the Throne, and its feet are in the depths of the seventh earth. It has a 
wing in the east and a wing in the west. The earthly roosters do not crow until this angel 
crows. When it crows, it flaps its wings and says,  
'Glory be to Allah {SWT}, Glory be to Allah {SWT}, Glory be to Allah {SWT}, the Great, who is 
unlike anything.'  
Allah {SWT}, Blessed and Exalted, responds and says; 
'One who knows what you say will not swear falsely by Me.'".  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 482 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 26, p. 505 • Tafsir Nur Al-Thaqalayn, 
Part 3, p. 613 • Tafseer Kanz Al-Daqaiq, Vol. 9, p. 325 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 67, H. 1395
reference.chapter: THE SAYING WHEN THE ROOSTER CROWS
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE SAYING WHEN THE ROOSTER CROWS
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٧، ح ١٣٩٥

hadiths.chapter: 67. THE SAYING WHEN THE ROOSTER CROWS
hadiths.ar:
1397   -  وَّ رُّوِى ز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ لِلط َّي ْزِ وَّ 
وَّإجِذ  عَّلَّى صُّورَّة ِ إَّلْأ َّسَّذِ ي َّسْي َّزْر ِق ُّ إَّلل َّهَّ ي َّعَّالَّى لِلسِّن َّاعِ وَّ وَّإجِذ  عَّلَّى صُّورَّة ِ إَّل ي  َّوْرِ ي َّسْي َّزْر ِق ُّ إَّلل َّهَّ ي َّعَّالَّى لِلْن َّهَّان ِمِ وَّ وَّإجِذ   
مِن ْهُّمْ عَّلَّى صُّورَّة ِ ن َّن ِى  إ ذَّمَّ ي َّسْي َّزْر ِق ُّ إَّلل َّهَّ ي َّعَّالَّى لِوُّلْذِ إ ذَّمَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّا ِذ َّإ كَّان َّ ي َّوْمُّ إَّ لْق ِي َّامَّه ِ صَّارُّوإ ي َّمَّان ِي َّه ً ق َّالَّ إَّلل َّهُّ 
عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ « :۝ وَّ ي َّحْمِلُّ عَّزْس َّ رَّن ِّك َّ ق َّوْق َّهُّمْ ي َّوْمَّن ِذ ٍ ي َّمٰان ِي َّه    ۝»
hadiths.en:
It is narrated: "Concerning this, the verse was revealed: 'And the birds with 
wings outspread; each knows its prayer and its glorification.'".  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 483 • Tafseer Kanz Al-Daqaiq, Vol. 9, p. 325 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 67, H. 1396

hadiths.chapter: 67. THE SAYING WHEN THE ROOSTER CROWS
hadiths.en:
It is narrated: "The carriers of the Throne today are four:  
One of them is in the form of a rooster, who seeks sustenance from Allah {SWT}, the 
Exalted, for the birds;  
Another is in the form of a lion, who seeks sustenance from Allah {SWT}, the Exalted, for 
the wild animals;  
Another is in the form of a bull, who seeks sustenance from Allah {SWT}, the Exalted, for 
the livestock;  
And one is in the form of a human being, who seeks sustenance from Allah {SWT}, the 
Exalted, for the children of Adam.  
On the Day of Resurrection, they will become eight, as Allah {SWT}, the Exalted, has said:  
'And the Throne of your Lord {AZJ} will be borne above them, on that Day, by eight' (Surah 
Al-Haqqah 69:17)." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 483 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 
26, p. 506

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 67, H. 1397

reference.location: Volume 1 › THE SAYING WHEN THE ROOSTER CROWS

hadiths.chapter: 68. THE SAYING WHEN STANDING UP FOR THE NIGHT
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"When you wish to stand for the night prayer, say:  
'O’ Allah {SWT}, I turn to You by Your Prophet, the Prophet of Mercy, and his family, and I 
present them before my needs. Make me, through them, 'honorable in this world and 
the Hereafter, and among those brought near (to You).'  
‘ O’ Allah {SWT}, have mercy on me through them, do not punish me through them, guide 
me through them, do not lead me astray through them, provide for me through them, 
and do not deprive me through them. Fulfil my needs for this world and the Hereafter, 
for indeed, You have power over all things and You have knowledge of all things.'" 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 483 • Meftah al -
Falah, Part 1, p. 307 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 345 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 636 • Bihar Al -Anwar, Vol. 84, p. 243 • 
Mustadrak Al-Wasail, Vol. 4, p. 152

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 68, H. 1398
reference.chapter: THE SAYING WHEN STANDING UP FOR THE NIGHT
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE SAYING WHEN STANDING UP FOR THE NIGHT
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٨، ح ١٣٩٨

hadiths.chapter: 70. THE NIGHT PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1400   -  وَّ رُّوِى حَّمْذَّ مَّز َّة ً و 
إَّللّٰهُّ إ َّجَّذ   ي َّلاَّي ِي ن َّ مَّز َّة ً إِن ْق َّي َّلَّ وَّ لَّي ْسَّ ن َّي ْن َّهُّ وَّ ن َّي ْن َّ إَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ ذ َّن ْث   إ ِلأ َّ ع َّق َّزَّ لَّهُّ . 
وي ق ز ق ى  ركعي ى  إلس ق غ وركعه  إلوي ز ق ل هو إلله إ جذ، وإق صل ن ي ن  إلس ق غ وإلوي ز ن ي سلي مه  .
hadiths.en:
The Prophet {saws} said in his advice to Imam Ali {a.s}: "O’ Ali, adhere to the night 
prayer, adhere to the night prayer, adhere to the night prayer”.  
[AL SADUQ] 
When you intend to perform it, begin by saying "Allahu Akbar" (God is the Greatest) 
seven times, praise Him seven times, then recite the opening supplication (Du'a 
Iftitah), and proceed to pray two units (Rak'ahs).  
In the first Rak'ah, recite Surah Al-Fatiha followed by Surah Al-Ikhlas ("Say: He is Allah 
{SWT}, the One").  
In the second Rak'ah, recite Surah Al -Fatiha followed by Surah Al -Kafirun ("Say: O’ 
disbelievers"). For the remaining six Rak'ahs, you may recite whatever you wish; you 
may lengthen or shorten as you like.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 70, H. 1399
reference.chapter: THE NIGHT PRAYER
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE NIGHT PRAYER
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٧٠، ح ١٣٩٩

hadiths.chapter: 70. THE NIGHT PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1401   -  وَّ رُّوِى :  «  ق ِي لَّ لَّهُّ إ َّن ْس ِزْ ي َّا عَّن ْذَّ إَّلل َّهِ ق َّق َّذْ ق َّن ِلَّ إَّلل َّه 
وَّي ْزَّك َّ .  
وإلق ن وب  ق ى  كل ركعي ي ن  ق ى  إلي ان ي ه  ق ن ل إلزكوع ون عذ إلق زإءة ، وإلق زإءة  ن ها ج هارإ. وإلق ن وب  ق ى  إلوي ز ق ن ل إلزكوع. وإ ن  ق مث  
ولم ي كن  علي ك  من  إلوق ث  ن ق ذر ما ي صلى  ق ي ه صلاة  إللي ل على ما ي زي ذ ق صلها وإ ذرج ها إ ذرإج ا ، وإلأذرإح  إ ن  ي ق زإ  ق ى  كل 
ركعه  إلحمذ وجذها، ق ا ن  ج س ي ث  طلوع إل ق ح ز ق صل ركعي ي ن  وإ وي ز ن الي الي ه ، وإ ن  طلغ إلق ح ز ق صل ركعي ى  إلق ح ز وق ذ مص ى  
إلوق ث  ن ما ق ي ه. وإ ذ إ صلي ث  من  صلاة  إللي ل إ رن غ ركعاب  من  ق ن ل طلوع إلق ح ز ق ا ي م إلصلاة  طلغ إلق ح ز إ و لم ي طلغ. وق ذ روي ث  
رج صه  ق ى  إ ن  ي صلى  إلزج ل صلاة  إللي ل ن عذ طلوع إلق ح ز إلمزة  ن عذ إلمزة ، ولأ ي ي ح ذ  ذ ل ك  عاذة . وإ ذ إ كان  علي ك  ق ص اء صلاة   
إللي ل ق ق مث  وعلي ك  من  إلوق ث  ن ق ذر ما ي صلى  إلق ان ي ه  وصلاة  لي لي ك  ق ان ذإ  ن الق ان ي ه  ق صل ي م صل صلاة  لي لي ك ، ق ا ن  كان  إلوق ث  
ن ق ذر ما ي صلى  وإجذة  ق صل صلاة  لي لي ك  لن لا ي صي زإ ج مي عا ق ص اء، ي م إق ض  إلصلاة  إلق ان ي ه  من  إلع ذ إ و ن عذ ذ لك .
hadiths.en:
It is narrated:  
"Whoever recites Surah Al -Fatiha once and 'Qul Huwa Allahu Ahad'  (Surah Al-Ikhlas) 
thirty times in each of the first two Rak'ahs of the night prayer, he will finish his prayer 
without any sin remaining between him and Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Glorious, except 
that He forgives it”. 
[AL SADUQ] 
In the two Rak'ahs of Shaf' (a pair prayer) and the single Rak'ah of Witr, recite 'Qul 
Huwa Allahu Ahad' (Surah Al-Ikhlas), and separate between the Shaf' and Witr prayers 
with the concluding Tasleem (salutation of peace). 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 485 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 2, p. 124 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 
8, p. 668 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 6, p. 129 • Tafsir Al-Burhan, vol. 5, p. 796 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 70, H. 1400
reference.chapter: THE NIGHT PRAYER

reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٧٠، ح ١٤٠٠

hadiths.chapter: 70. THE NIGHT PRAYER
hadiths.en:
It is narrated :  
"Whoever recites in the Witr prayer the two protective chapters (Surah Al -Falaq and 
Surah An-Nas) and 'Qul Huwa Allahu Ahad' (Surah Al-Ikhlas), it will be said to him,  
'Rejoice, O’ servant of Allah {SWT}, for Allah {SWT} has accepted your Witr.'"  
[AL SADUQ] 
The Qunoot (supplication) is to be performed in every two Rak'ahs, specifically in the 
second Rak'ah, before bowing (Ruku') and after the recitation, and it should be done 
aloud. The Qunoot is also performed in the Witr prayer before bowing.  
If you stand for prayer but do not have enough time to perform the entire night prayer 
as intended, then perform it in a summarized form, meaning recite only Surah Al -
Fatiha in each Rak'ah. If you fear the approach of Fajr, perform two Rak'ahs and 
complete the Witr with a third. If Fajr has begun, perform the two Rak'ahs of Fajr 
prayer as soon as time permits. 
If you have managed to pray four Rak'ahs of the night prayer before Fajr, continue 
and complete the remaining prayers, whether Fajr has begun or not. There is a 
concession for offering the night prayer after Fajr has begun on occasion, but it 
should not become habitual. 
If you have missed the night prayer and have enough time upon waking to perform both 
the missed and current night's prayer, begin with the missed prayer and then perform 
the current one. If there is only enough time for one, perform the current night praye r 
so that neither becomes overdue, and then make up the missed prayer later, either on 
the next day or thereafter. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 485 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 
8, p. 668

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 70, H. 1401
